title: Warm up
content: This slide introduces three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—through illustrated images of taxi drivers navigating each city, each depicted with distinct visual themes reflecting technology, tradition, and scenery.
key_claims: Beijing is depicted with modern highways, city skyline, and traditional architecture blending technology.
Chongqing is shown with mountainous terraced architecture and a cable car scene.
Hangzhou is illustrated with a lake, greenery, and futuristic AI/technology elements.

title: An Evolving Nation: A Ride Through China
content: This is a title slide introducing the presentation's theme, using taxi and Chinese cityscape imagery to set the stage for exploring China's urban development through a metaphorical journey.
key_claims: The presentation's theme is 'An Evolving Nation: A Ride Through China.'
Imagery includes a taxi sign and iconic Chinese architecture (Forbidden City, illuminated buildings, pagoda).

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines the first session's learning framework, presenting a three-step process for investigating city development: identifying local features, formulating development strategies, and analyzing outcomes and positive impacts.
key_claims: The investigative process follows three sequential steps: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact.
Session 1 focuses on research and investigation skills.

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This slide presents a matching activity where students connect three Chinese cities (Beijing, Chongqing, Hangzhou) to their corresponding development strategies (Technology & tradition integration, Sustainable urban development, Tourism & Economic growth).
key_claims: Beijing matches with 'Sustainable urban development' based on arrow connections.
Chongqing matches with 'Technology & tradition integration.'
Hangzhou matches with 'Tourism & Economic growth.'

title: Session 2 Ask & Analyse: critical analysis & comparative insights
content: This slide details Session 2's task instructions, dividing students into two roles: 'city observers' who select and ask questions, and 'city experts' who answer using text examples, fostering critical analysis and comparison.
key_claims: City observers select 1 question from a list to ask each group.
City experts must answer questions using text examples.
The session focuses on critical analysis and comparative insights between cities.

title: Session 3 Promote Your City!
content: This slide describes a role-play activity where groups act as 'City Promotion Ambassadors' to create a 2-minute promotional speech for their assigned city, incorporating specific roles and content requirements.
key_claims: Groups must create a 2-minute promotional speech as City Promotion Ambassadors (城市宣传大使).
Taxi drivers share a fun story about the city; city experts explain strategies.
Speeches must use at least 3 key phrases from the text and end with a creative slogan.

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This summary slide connects each city's taxi driver imagery to its corresponding development strategy, reinforcing the overarching theme that China's cities tailor development approaches to local characteristics.
key_claims: Beijing represents sustainable urban development.
Chongqing represents tourism & economic growth.
Hangzhou represents technology & tradition integration.
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote development, reflecting the principle of 因地制宜 (adapting measures to local conditions).
